summary: Any product row can independently change the result membership, so exact filtering has a linear inspection lower bound; one predicate scan matches that bound.

optimality.argument: An uninspected row may be the only row with both flags equal to Y, so omitting any row can omit a required product identifier. Evaluating both constant-time predicates once per row takes O(R) time and matches the lower bound.

replacement_checks.evidence: Changing either flag of any uninspected product can change whether its unique product_id must appear.
The query performs one scan, two equality checks per row, and projects each qualifying primary key once.
Authored fixtures cover all four flag combinations, empty and complete result sets, each one-flag exclusion, one match, unsorted identifiers, and mixed qualifying and nonqualifying rows.
